title: Remove the Controller PCA
summary: Safely access the controller box and disconnect the Controller PCA for replacement removal. This source-specific runbook covers the documented removal portion only: lockout/tagout, required PPE and tools, optional station side panel removal for access, controller box release, ribbon cable retainer removal, and disconnecting the FFC, ribbon cable, and three Molex connectors from the Controller PCA.
when_to_use: Use when performing the documented removal portion of the Controller PCA replacement procedure in the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual, after the tipper has been locked out/tagged out and access to the controller box is required.
not_for: Reinstallation or verification steps, which are not present in this source packet.
Use when the access hardware, fasteners, or connectors do not match the source description.

responsibilities: Perform LOTO on the tipper before starting removal.
Use the documented PPE and tools.
Remove access hardware as needed, release the controller box, remove the ribbon cable retainer, and disconnect the documented PCA connections.
Stop and escalate if hardware or connectors do not match the source description or cannot be safely disconnected.

safety_notes: LOTO the tipper before starting Controller PCA removal.
Do not remove the four M8 button-head fasteners when releasing the controller box; loosen them and lift the box off the loosened fasteners.
This procedure includes component removal and is not suitable for general operator execution.
access_or_tools_needed: Physical access to the station side panel and controller box

access_required: Physical access to the station side panel and controller box
system_preconditions: Tipper is locked out/tagged out before removal begins.

visual_references.description: Controller PCA figure for the documented removal procedure and connector area reference.
Controller box location reference showing the controller/controller box context for release and access.

steps.title: LOTO the tipper
steps.purpose: Establish a safe condition before opening access panels and disconnecting Controller PCA connections.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper as referenced by the manual before starting Controller PCA removal.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is in a locked out/tagged out state and safe for the documented removal work to begin.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO has been applied before any panel removal or connector disconnection begins.
steps.failure_condition: LOTO has not been applied or cannot be confirmed before starting work.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper before starting Controller PCA removal.

stop_or_escalate_if: LOTO cannot be completed or verified before proceeding.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby procedure text states required tools include a 1.5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench or socket, with replacement part Controller PCA and PPE of safety glasses and gloves. Removal steps instruct the technician to LOTO the tipper.

steps.title: Gather PPE and tools
steps.purpose: Ensure the documented protective equipment and tools are available before disassembly starts.
steps.instruction: Gather the documented PPE and tools: safety glasses, gloves, 1.5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench or socket.
steps.expected_result: All documented PPE and tools are available at the work area before removal continues.
steps.healthy_condition: Safety glasses, gloves, and all listed tools are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: Required PPE or any documented tool is missing.

actions.label: Verify PPE and tools
actions.exact_action: Gather the documented PPE and tools: safety glasses, gloves, 1.5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench or socket.

stop_or_escalate_if: Required PPE or tools are not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby procedure text states required tools include a 1.5-mm hex wrench, 6-mm hex wrench, and 3/8-in. wrench or socket, with replacement part Controller PCA and PPE of safety glasses and gloves.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if needed
steps.purpose: Gain access to the motor and internal area needed for Controller PCA removal when the side panel blocks access.
steps.instruction: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the station side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed when needed and access to the motor/internal area is available.
steps.healthy_condition: The side panel is removed cleanly and the access area is open.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed, the fasteners do not match the documented description, or access remains blocked.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the station side panel.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Controller/controller box location and surrounding access context after side panel removal.
Controller PCA procedure figure for the controller area associated with this removal task.

stop_or_escalate_if: The documented access hardware or fasteners do not match the source description.
The side panel cannot be safely removed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Removal steps instruct the technician to remove nine self-tapping fasteners from the station side panel if needed.
Nearby text states the procedure instructs technicians to LOTO the tipper, remove the station side panel if needed, and loosen four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box.

steps.title: Release the controller box
steps.purpose: Free the controller box so the Controller PCA and its connections can be accessed.
steps.instruction: Loosen the four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box; do not remove the fasteners, and lift the box off the loosened fasteners.
steps.expected_result: The controller box is released and lifted off the loosened fasteners without removing them.
steps.healthy_condition: The controller box lifts free after the four M8 button-head screws are loosened, and the fasteners remain in place.
steps.failure_condition: The controller box cannot be lifted off the loosened fasteners, or the securing points do not match the documented description.

actions.label: Loosen controller box screws
actions.exact_action: Loosen the four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box; do not remove the fasteners.

actions.label: Lift controller box free
actions.exact_action: Lift the controller box off the loosened fasteners.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Controller box location and mounting context associated with releasing the box.
Controller PCA figure for the controller box area used in this procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The controller box cannot be lifted off the loosened fasteners.
The mounting hardware or securing points do not match the source description.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Removal steps instruct the technician to loosen four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box.
The procedure instructs technicians to loosen four M8 button-head screws to release the controller box.

steps.title: Remove the ribbon cable retainer
steps.purpose: Remove the retainer so the ribbon cable connection can be disconnected from the Controller PCA.
steps.instruction: Remove the two M3 socket-head screws holding the ribbon cable retainer in place.
steps.expected_result: The ribbon cable retainer is removed and the ribbon cable connection is accessible.
steps.healthy_condition: Both M3 socket-head screws are removed and the retainer is free.
steps.failure_condition: The retainer cannot be removed or the hardware does not match the documented description.

actions.label: Remove retainer screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the two M3 socket-head screws holding the ribbon cable retainer in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Controller PCA connection area where the ribbon cable retainer is located.

stop_or_escalate_if: The retainer hardware does not match the source description.
The retainer cannot be safely removed.

steps.title: Disconnect the Controller PCA connections
steps.purpose: Fully disconnect the Controller PCA from its documented cables and connectors so it is ready for the next replacement step.
steps.instruction: Unplug all documented connections from the PCA: the FFC, the ribbon cable, and the three Molex connectors.
steps.expected_result: The Controller PCA is disconnected from the FFC, ribbon cable, and three Molex connectors and is ready for the next replacement step.
steps.healthy_condition: All documented PCA connections are unplugged without unresolved attachment points remaining.
steps.failure_condition: Any PCA connection cannot be safely disconnected, or the connectors do not match the source description.

actions.label: Disconnect FFC
actions.exact_action: Unplug the FFC from the PCA.

actions.label: Disconnect ribbon cable
actions.exact_action: Unplug the ribbon cable from the PCA.

actions.label: Disconnect Molex connectors
actions.exact_action: Unplug the three Molex connectors from the PCA.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Controller PCA connection area and the documented FFC, ribbon cable, and Molex connector locations.
FFC-related connection hardware context for identifying the flat-flex cable style referenced in the procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: Any PCA connection cannot be safely disconnected.
The documented connectors do not match the source description.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-19 appears on page 177 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_18_1_removal.

success_criteria: The tipper was locked out/tagged out before removal work began.
The documented PPE and tools were used.
The station side panel was removed if needed for access.
The controller box was released by loosening the four M8 button-head screws without removing them.
The ribbon cable retainer was removed by taking out the two M3 socket-head screws.
The Controller PCA was disconnected from the FFC, ribbon cable, and three Molex connectors.
The Controller PCA is ready for the next replacement step.
healthy_conditions: LOTO is in place before disassembly.
Access hardware and connectors match the source description.
The controller box lifts off the loosened fasteners as described.
All documented PCA connections disconnect cleanly.
failure_conditions: LOTO is not completed before work begins.
Required PPE or tools are missing.
Access hardware, fasteners, or connectors do not match the source description.
The controller box cannot be lifted off the loosened fasteners.
The ribbon cable retainer cannot be removed.
Any PCA connection cannot be safely disconnected.
escalation_guidance: Stop if the documented access hardware, fasteners, or connectors do not match the source description.
Escalate if the controller box cannot be lifted off the loosened fasteners.
Escalate if any PCA connection cannot be safely disconnected.
Do not use this procedure for general operator execution.
